LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CHRISTINE HAY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT WAS SERVED WITH THE DEED IN THE DECLARATION OF DISCLOSURES AND THUS RECEIVED ADEQUATE NOTICE. PROOF OF SERVICE FOR THE DECLARATION WAS FILED WITH THE COURT. THE SUBSTANCE OF THE NOTICE REQUIREMENT OF FAMILY CODE SEC. 2040(B)(3) WAS ADEQUATELY FULFILLED. FURTHERMORE, FAMILY CODE SEC. 2040 DOES NOT APPEAR TO MANDATE THE HARSH RESULT OF VOIDING THE SEVERANCE. THE 7/28/2008 DEED ADEQUATELY DESCRIBED THE SUBJECT PROPERTY IN COMPLIANCE WITH CC SEC. 1092. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
MINI-MINUTES FOR MAY-28-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-01-2009 Set For Trial On Jun-28-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Jun-10-10 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 613

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar May-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CHRISTINE HAY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied, failure to shift burden of proof. Defendant was served with the deed in the Declaration of Disclosures and thus received adequate notice. Proof of service for the Declaration was filed with the Court. The substance of the notice requirement of Family Code sec. 2040(b)(3) was adequately fulfilled. Furthermore, Family Code sec. 2040 does not appear to mandate the harsh result of voiding the severance. The 7/28/2008 deed adequately described the subject property in compliance with CC sec. 1092. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-01-2009 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Compel Answers To Interrogatories; For Order Establishing Admissions; And Responses To Inspection Demands And Production Of Documents; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions;
Off Calendar Mar-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF DAVID LING's Motion To Compel Answers To Interrogatories, For An Order Establishing Admissions, And Responses To Inspection Demands And Production Of Documents is moot as responses have been served. Defendant is ordered to produce responsive documents within 15 days of notice of this order. The late responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $440, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.
